description_long: This house features a rustic, timber-framed exterior with a combination of stone and wood materials, giving it a traditional European countryside appearance. The roof is steeply pitched and covered in dark shingles, with visible moss and weathering that suggest age and character. The front facade includes a central wooden door flanked by large, multi-pane windows and accented by exposed wooden beams. Three dormer windows protrude from the main roof, adding architectural interest and natural light to the upper level. The side of the house extends into a covered porch area supported by wooden posts, ideal for outdoor seating or entry. The stone foundation and lower walls provide a sturdy base, while the overall structure is elongated with a prominent gable roof and cross-gabled section.
description: A rustic, timber-framed house with a steep, dark shingled roof, stone foundation, and prominent dormer windows. The design features exposed beams, large windows, and a covered porch area.
description_view_0: This view highlights the long side of the house with three dormer windows, a covered porch, and a stone foundation.
description_view_1: The front facade is visible, showing the main entry door, large windows, and decorative timber framing.
description_view_2: The side view reveals the cross-gabled roof structure, exposed beams, and a mix of stone and timber walls.
description_view_3: The rear of the house is shown, with a simpler wall design, fewer windows, and the extension of the covered porch.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows with dormers and large front windows
attribute_entryway_design: Central wooden door with stone steps, flanked by large windows and exposed timber framing, no prominent porch but a covered side area
